records.ety: [OE. widewe, widwe, AS. weoduwe, widuwe, wuduwe; akin to OFries. widwe, OS. widowa, D. weduwe, G. wittwe, witwe, OHG. wituwa, witawa, Goth. widuwō, Russ. udova, OIr. fedb, W. gweddw, L. vidua, Skr. vidhavā; and probably to Skr. vidh to be empty, to lack; cf. Gr. "hi`qeos a bachelor. √248. Cf. Vidual.]

defs: In various games (such as “hearts”), any extra hand or part of a hand, as one dealt to the table. It may be taken by one of the players under certain circumstances.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Widowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Widowing.]

defs: To reduce to the condition of a widow; to bereave of a husband; -- rarely used except in the past participle.
quotes: Though in thus city he Hath widowed and unchilded many a one, Which to this hour bewail the injury.

defs: To deprive of one who is loved; to strip of anything beloved or highly esteemed; to make desolate or bare; to bereave.
quotes: The widowed isle, in mourning, Dries up her tears.
Tress of their shriveled fruits Are widowed, dreary storms o'er all prevail.
Mourn, widowed queen; forgotten Sion, mourn.

defs: To endow with a widow's right.
To become, or survive as, the widow of.
quotes: Let me be married to three kings in a forenoon, and widow them all.
